Secret Sorceries
of Mind-Control
Copy
Part 1: Seven 	Secrets of
Mind-Control Copy
With Dan Kennedy
www.gkic.com
www.getwsodownload.com

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	3
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Seven 	Secrets 	of Mind-Control Copy
In this module, we're going to 	deal with seven secrets of mind-control copy for the
selling of info-products and services.
The preface remark is this; it is possible that some of you will be made
uncomfortable. More so those of you 	who are the “virgins” to this than the ones
who've been hanging around for a while.
It is possible that some people will be made uncomfortable by some of the
techniques within these seven secrets because the underlying premise is one of the
most dangerous 	premises ever invented by man.
It is that “the end justifies the means”.
In many cases, that has not worked out very well; 	but it is necessary to sell to the
maximum 	– 	and 	to use the most powerful techniques there are in copy to sell info-
products 	– 	for 	you to feel okay in your situation with
the end justifying your means.
It is beyond me to have a big ethics class with you
but I will just forewarn you that I am acting under
the assumption that everybody reading this is an
honorable ethical person who is selling good stuff
that will be so beneficial to people that every
extreme mechanism used to sell it is a good thing.
If that's not true, it's up to you to figure out how far to back off from the maximum
effectiveness.
The reason extreme techniques are more necessary
in this business than in many is that we actually are,
to a great extent, selling nothing.
It is possible that
some people will be
made uncomfortable
by some of the
techniques
Page 1 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	4
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
We are essentially selling people on their own assets, skills, talents, capabilities and
opportunities that 	– 	through whatever it is we do 	– 	we 	improve their health, we
improve their wealth, we improve whatever, that they are going to be better at that.
Actually, when they invest with you,
they are really 	investing in themselves.
It is not like selling a magic slicer, dicer machine where there's a machine and, for
your money, 	you actually walk out of the room with a machine.
In our case, if you walk out with anything, you walk out with “stuff” that has to 	be
translated into action in order for it to have any value.
So our job is much tougher and that requires us to use tougher techniques.
Four Vital Principles
To start with, 	there are four vital principles.
 	Number one; 	is in selling; 	in copy that sells information, we, ironically,
do not empower the prospect to think.
In the selling phase of what we do, 	we are not in the business of getting
anybody to think at all. In fact better that
they don't.
We are not empowering them; 	we are actually
making them 	powerless 	for a brief period of
time to engage in any thinking except the
thinking that we direct them 	to do, 	that
serves our purpose 	– 	which is selling them
what it is that we are there, by media, in
person, however it is that we are there, to sell them.
 	Number 	two; we do not seek rational thought. 	We seek EMOTIONAL
RESPONSE
In the selling phase,
we 	are not in the
business of getting
anybody to
think at all
Page 2 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	5
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
We seek emotional response at the exclusion of rational thought.
Our buyers, for the most part, have to suspend logic, they have to suspend
skepticism, 	and they 	have to suspend their past experience, often in order to
make a purchase from us.
To 	do that, 	we are all about emotional response,
not at all about logic.
Everything that even appears to be logical, and I'll point it out in the copy
examples 	as we go along, is really all about an emotional response.
 	Very important, 	Number 	three;
ironically, we do not educate any more
than is absolutely necessary to make
the sale.
We manipulate, we motivate.
We 	do not need 	an informed consumer;
we need an 	EXCITED 	consumer.
One of the biggest copy mistakes in this
business is dispensing information and
education and being a teacher 	– 	instead
of being a sales person up until the time
that we have their money and now we
should begin to teach.
So there is a tendency 	– 	speakers make the mistake a lot but it transfers into
media and it transfers into copy 	– 	there is a tendency, for example, if you
have a marketing document that you call a 	Special 	Report.
The 	Four Vital Principles
1. We do NOT want 	to empower them to
think. We want them POWERLESS to think in
any way but the way most conducive to our
making a sale.
2. We do NOT seek rational thought. We seek
EMOTIONAL response.
3. We do NOT seek to educate. We
manipulate and motivate. We do NOT need
an informed consumer. We need an EXCITED
consumer.
4. There is always a SALES purpose behind
every piece of copy.
Page 3 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	6
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
When 	you offer and deliver a report to somebody, there is a tendency for
people to think that 	– 	because they call it a report 	– 	it's supposed to have
information in it.
o 	It 	is supposed to appear to have information in it.
o 	It is supposed to be perceived as having information in it.
o 	It is supposed to be perceived as being educational.
If it is actually educational and informative, 	all that does is get in the way of
the sale.
We 	are about selling stuff first, educating after the
sale 	– 	not 	as part of the sale.
 	Number Four; 	there is a sales purpose behind 	every 	single piece of
copy…. Almost always tied to an “E-Factor”
If it does not further the sale:
o 	It does not earn its space on the page.
o 	It 	does not earn its minutes in 	the video.
o 	It 	does not earn its minutes on stage.
Speakers, for example, will get bored with what they do 	and the temptation is
to put in new stuff, to talk about something topical that interests them.
I did that in a previous session where I 	referenced 	the CNN interview with
Zuckerberg's sister.
That's only permissible because I had no purpose other than to fill time, be,
perhaps, informative, entertain, have everybody be happy.
If I had been trying to sell something, 	that would be an unforgivable sin
because there was nothing about it that would've advanced the sale.
Page 4 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	7
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
In 	order to do it today I would have had to take something out of that script
that was there already that advanced the sale to make room for it. So, 	in
indulging myself, 	I would be jeopardizing the sale.
People do the same thing in media. They tend to put in a story that they like
to tell but it really doesn't move the sale forward. We don't have that luxury.
Everything is about moving forward the sale and
it is almost always tied 	to an emotional factor 	(or e-factor).
There 	are some lists that exist for you of the basic e-factors; one 	place to find them is
in the 	No B.S, 	Marketing to the Affluent book.
The most basic e-factors are:
 	Fear
 	Guilt
 	Greed
 	Pride
 	Love 	or, if you prefer, 	lust
It sort of depends on whether you're on your first marriage, second marriage, fifth
marriage 	– 	where you are in that whole continuum!
Then, also in that book, you'll find some e-factors that happen to be specific to
affluent individuals. Whatever your target market, 	there will be e-factors specific to
that target market but the big general ones apply.
Importantly, we do better with the dark than with the light and most of what you
will see 	here 	is in the dark category not in the light category.
I 	will 	convincingly demonstrate to you that, 	if you have a choice 	– 	and 	you are not
able to split test 	– 	you are always better off dragging them into the darkness than
you are showing them happy things and light.
Page 5 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	8
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
You can view the full ad as Example
#1 in the Exhibits
Secret 	#1: Shame
So, we begin with the first of 	the seven 	secrets. It is shame. No-one wants to live in
Loserville.
There are a whole lot of people who don't want to do
enough about it to get out and we tend not to get money
from them but no-one wants to be there at all.
Our buyer has a stronger reaction to it than most. He
fears and loathes it and he can be motivated to do
something about it.
This is called negative motivation and of all of the options
for 	how to sell people on improvement, 	opportunity, education, information, change,
negative motivation works so much better than positive motivation.
Here's my favorite shame ad. This is from 1923. It's the only one I think I'll show
you 	here that's not actually an information
marketing industry example but it is so good I had
to show it to you.
This actually is for a paint and varnish products
company that sells paint to paint your house with.
The ad shows 	two people standing outside a house
and the headline is:
"Jones must be broke."
The 	copy is all about his house looks so bad, clearly
the poor sucker must be broke. That's the only
reason he has let his house have chipped paint and
so forth.
1. SHAME
Ambition to escape the
unwanted is available in a
larger number of people
(than positive motivation)
and easier to stimulate than
is ambition for gain or
benefit.
Page 6 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	9
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
It is an ad to get you to send in a coupon to get coupons to buy paint with.
You can't have a 	stronger shame headline than 	“Jones 	Must be 	Broke.”
1923, great copywriter.
Confidence, consideration and respect surrounds the family whose home, 	inside
and out, 	bears the look of prosperity that only the proper use of paint and
varnish can impart.
Isn't that good?
Problems with Our Business
There's three chief problems with our business. 	We're only going to deal with one
here 	but just so you know what all three of them are…
Everybody understands the first one. 	The first chief evil of our business is the cost
of new customer acquisition.
That's the first chief problem in our business that everybody is bedeviled by. If
you're not yet bedeviled by it, you will be as soon as you try and get enough growth
and it manifests itself all different ways:
 	The 	cost of filling a seat in a preview seminar
 	The 	cost of getting traffic to a site in sufficient quantity that you can build a
business from it 	etc., etc.
The second evil is the ever-rising clutter and competition of 	free content. That's a
topic not for 	here; it's just so you know it's the second evil.
The third is one a lot of people don't understand and it's very hard for 	many people
reading this to 	get; 	that is the absence of legitimate ambition in the overwhelming
majority of every population.
Page 7 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	10
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
If 	you sell to business owners, for example, and you are a business owner, you are an
ambitious business owner.
You’ve 	made sacrifices because you are ambitious. You get up pretty much every
day being ambitious.
It's 	very hard for you to think that there's a bunch of people
in business 	– 	or 	a bunch of people in your business 	–
who, in fact, are not ambitious at all.
They may speak ambition but they don't do ambition and we, mostly, can only make
money on people who do ambition.
In business owners there's three categories. There’s 	the be-your-own-boss crowd.
That's the people who really just want not to have a
boss.
So that's often, say, the auto mechanic who has
become the owner of an auto repair shop.
He did not do that because he had ambition. His
ambition was limited to not having a boss.
He has the delusion that, 	if he just owned the thing instead of working 	for the guy
that owned the thing, he would be free of a boss.
He discovers, of course, to his dire disappointment, 	that he now has a thousand
bosses instead of one 	– 	because they are his customers 	– 	and 	he has one primary
boss who's completely dysfunctional and incompetent and he also has a really
crappy employee.
They are 	one and 	the same; they are him.
He can't quit and he can't fire him.
We, mostly, can only
make money on
people who do
ambition.
Page 8 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	11
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
So, he's in a world of hurt but that does not translate into the kind of ambition that
we get to leverage.
The overwhelming majority can be more motivated by ambition to escape the
unwanted than ambition to grow, improve, excel, build an empire, be an
entrepreneur, be healthy, be fit etc.
So it's important you 	build into your messaging things that are related to the
ambition to escape the unwanted.
It exists waiting to be poked in a greater number of people and it is easier to
stimulate 	in a greater number of people. 	So we have to use it.
If you only sell to one percenters it's very hard to make that business work.
In fact, the difference between even high income individuals and wealthy individuals
is high income individuals only monetize their customers, wealthy people monetize
all their unconverted leads, they monetize everybody who they touch, everybody
who touches them, one way or another.
In order for us to do that, we have to appeal to the actual ambition that a large
percentage 	of the people we talk to, write 	to, 	communicate with start out with 	–
which is the ambition to escape the unwanted 	– 	not 	the ambition to be better, to be
bigger, to be richer, to 	be healthier, to be thinner.
It is the ambition to escape the unwanted.
If you wanted 	a Thesaurus 	for it, here it is:
 	People don't want to be left out, they don't want to be left behind
 	They don't want to be uncool
 	They 	don't want to be stupid
 	They 	don't want to be attached to a loser or a losing argument
Page 9 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	12
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
This 	is why in politics, by the way, the perception of who's going to win begins to
affect how people vote dramatically. So, all the so-called undecided voters, that's
why they're undecided.
They're not undecided because they haven't yet got enough information in order to
make a decision.
It's two weeks from the election; our election cycles 	are now permanent; 	we're in
the 2016 presidential election cycle now.
By the time we get to two weeks before the election, 	how could any person possibly
be 	undecided 	but you hear about all these undecided voters.
Well, this is what they're undecided about; they're undecided about who's going to
win because they want to vote for the winner so they can say they picked the
winner. They don't want to 	be associated with the loser.
There are exceptions. There's a guy in my neighborhood who's got a Jimmy Carter
bumper sticker on his car but he's an exception. You've got to give him credit for
being stubborn.
 	People don't want to be embarrassed.
 	They don't want to feel foolish.
 	They 	don't want to feel incompetent.
 	They don't want to feel weak and impotent.
 	They don't want to be looked down upon.
 	They don't want to be unwanted, pitied, gossiped about, unattractive or old.
They 	have ambition to escape these conditions and they are far easier to motivate
about that escape than they are about the positive alternatives to all of this.
It's also important to understand that, 	even if you sell to successful people, they are
hyper-sensitive to criticism and imagined critical thoughts of others.
Page 10 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	13
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
You can see the full ad as Example #2 in the
Exhibits.
Almost everybody's walking around wondering what somebody else
and somebodies else is thinking about them.
For the most part, nobody's thinking about them at all.
So, most of this is in their imagination, it's not in reality but it is there. They are
wondering about that and so you can use it if you wish.
I'll 	show you a couple of classics. This is from 1922. You could tweak it and run it
today.
How 	Little Social Errors Ruined Their Biggest Chance
This is a full page ad, ran in newspapers in the 1920s.
It's got a coupon in it. It's a lead generation ad for a
home study course.
I'm just going to read you a few of the sub-heads 	and
the captions.
Bad mistakes are made. The 	Crayton’s 	suffered
keen 	humiliation. He 	knew 	that others were
watching them, reading in their
embarrassment, their lack of social knowledge.
But then something happened. Violet noticed
that 	Mrs. 	Roberts had glanced at her husband
and frowned ever so slightly. She wondered
what was wrong.
Perhaps it was incorrect to cut lettuce with a
knife. Perhaps Ted should not have used his fork that way. In her
embarrassment, she dropped her knife and bent down to pick it up at the same
time that the butler did.
Page 11 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	14
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Oh, it was humiliating! Unbearable! 	They should never have come. They didn't
know what to do. They did not know how to act.
You could tweak it. You could run it today.
Now, the truth of the matter is, of course, really, probably, nobody thought anything
of it but we think people think something of it.
That's why we own mirrors. If we weren't sensitive to this, 	we would just put on
what we wanted and go.
Here is an ad, this is not an information ad. I'm not sure how old this is but it's
ancient.
The photograph at the top is of a couple. Woman adoringly
looking at him and the guy 	kind of pathetic:
Turned 	down again
The problem, by the way, is a thing called comedones 	– 	or
blackheads.
What's more, you may not even be conscious of
comedones but your friends notice them.
You may wonder why invitations 	become fewer, why
friends, girls in particular, seem to avoid you. You may
never guess; 	perhaps it's comedones. Pompeian Massage
Cream 	helps you overcome comedones.
Key words: 	"You may wonder why invitations...” 	So you don't
even know.
This is how Listerine was sold to start with, by the way, too.
Bad breath was invented; 	the owners of Listerine invented the
condition.
You can see the full ad as
Example #3 in the Exhibits.
Page 12 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	15
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
You can see the full ad as Example #4 in the
Exhibits.
Here's a more recent 	ad 	for an information product. 	“Let 	Me Give You The Secrets
Of Fearless Conversation.”
There's an endorsement here by Larry King, by
the way.
Don't you hate it; the prospect of 	walking
into a room that's packed with strangers,
having to approach someone you've never
met and strike up a conversation?
It's natural to feel uncomfortable. You don't
know.
Are you making a positive first impression,
are you saying the right things?
The answer is this course. It might interest you
where this ad succeeded; Investor’s Business
Daily, the Wall Street Journal, Forbes; 	not Trailer
Park Monthly.
Point being, 	personal anxiety and hyper-sensitivity to criticism, the desire not to be
embarrassed, the desire not to be gossiped about, the desire not to feel foolish is not
exclusive to any demographic or psychographic.
The Forbes 400, the richest 	men and women in the world, get up every morning
worrying 	about 	this stuff just like anybody else does.
When 	they go into any environment they're not comfortable with, 	what they want to
know is; 	“How are you supposed to behave around here?”
Now, they do know, probably, which fork to use but they go into all sorts of
environments they don't know the answer.
Page 13 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	16
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
You can see the full ad as Example #5 	in the
Exhibits.
One of the 	all-time 	classic ads in our industry is 	"They Laughed When 	I 	Sat Down
At The Piano But When 	I 	Started To Play!"
I couldn't 	lay my hands on it, but there's a
version of this 	– 	Caples also wrote it 	– 	about
"They All Laughed When The Waiter Came
Over In The 	French 	Restaurant But Then
When 	I 	Started To Order In 	French".
It's not exactly that but it's pretty close. It's
the same premise; being laughed at, not
wanting to be laughed at.
The whole first part of this ad is all about
how he's being 	goaded 	into playing and really
they’re 	goading 	him into playing in order to
make fun of him.
Then I started to play. Instantly a tense
silence fell on the guests. The laughter
died on their lips as if by magic. I
played through the first few bars of
Beethoven's immortal Moonlight
Sonata. 	I heard gasps of amazement.
My friends sat breathless, spellbound.
The subhead is 	"A Complete 	Triumph."
Now, the truth of the matter is; who do you know who doesn't want to stick it up the
butts of some friends of theirs over some skepticism those friends have about them?
We all do.
This is one of the most reliable emotions there is.
Page 14 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	17
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
You can see the full ad as Example #6 in the Exhibits.
This is a successful ad of mine. The photograph is of a guy sitting in his underwear
talking on the phone and it is the 	famous 	"How You Can Make $4,000 A Day
Sitting At Your Kitchen Table In Your Underwear" 	ad.
I'll read you a piece of copy from it:
I went from a young, bright,
successful guy with a great job
to an embarrassment to my
family, a mooch to my friends.
I sat in that basement after
Peggy and the kids went to
sleep, on one hand feeling like
finding a bridge and jumping,
on the other hand more
determined than ever.
This is what people want.
They want to go from these
negative emotional
experiences to triumph.
 	They want that much more
than they want improvement
for improvement's sake.
 	They want it much more than they want opportunity for opportunity's sake.
 	They want it much more than they want wellness for wellness' sake.
They want to go from a collection 	of negative emotions and
experiences to triumph.
That's what we sell with our copy.
Page 15 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	18
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Secret 	#2: Emotional Pain
The second key factor 	is 	emotional pain. Powerful sales copy inflicts emotional pain.
We twist the knife.
If we can 	figure out what a target market's emotional pain is,
then we want to make it as painful for them as is humanly
possible.
A copy win is the person who read our copy and then could
not sleep because of the angst we created.
So, you've had an experience of intense emotional pain. For example:
 	You have failed miserably at something.
 	You have embarrassed yourself somewhere.
 	You have had a humiliating day or night and you’ve 	come home and you can't
get it out of your head.
That's exactly where I want the reader.
Then I, of course, want to solve it for him.
So the methods of twisting the knife, here are the main ones.
Familiar Scenarios
One 	is with an all too familiar 	scenario.
Here's a favorite cartoon of mine. They’re 	at the
altar and she is saying, 	"That's what you're
wearing?”
The headline is, 	"It starts."
Isn't that an all too familiar scenario?
2. EMOTIONAL PAIN
Powerful sales copy inflicts
emotional pain. Find it and
twist the knife.
Page 16 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	19
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
I had a first wife who did that a lot. You're going out the door, 	“Are you wearing that
tie with that suit?”
“No, I put it on for practice!” “No, I'm going to take it off in the car and go tie-less!”
Of course I am!
Right before you went out the door. 	All too familiar scenario!
Confessions
Next is personal stories and confessions the reader sharply identifies with.
This is copy for a client of mine who's a real estate coach who sells to other real
estate agents. It's in the middle of context of a lot of other copy:
The 	Day That Turned My Life Around
I looked at that trophy that said I was number one but I felt like an absolute
zero.
I sat in a near dark room alone, Catherine 	upstairs asleep, a storm pushing rain
against the window and occasionally illuminating the black sky with a
momentary bolt of light and I stared at that trophy and felt my hatred for it
deepening, my shame in having it wrenching. I stared at that trophy 	and cried.
I cursed and swore at that damn trophy. It lied, I wasn't number one, I was
number zero because I still had to work at everything as if starting out at zero.
The morning after being handed the trophy and every morning thereafter I had
to answer the same bell, do the same drudgery, face the same rejection.
I still had to apologize for interrupting dinner for another quick call that took
20 minutes. I still had to apologize for another Saturday at work.
I still had to worry about where and when the next commission check would
materialize.
Page 17 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	20
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
I still started each month with certain bills but uncertain income. I'd fought
and clawed and worked day and night to get that trophy but it changed
nothing.
That is a personal story that every successful real estate agent and pretty much any
successful sales person can identify with.
They're getting accolades, recognition, award pins, trophies, praise and yet, inside,
nothing has changed.
They're waking up in the morning facing
the same 	“Oh my God, where's the next
deal coming from?” 	emotion.
Now, it's embellished.
Why? Because I want it embellished in
his head.
You could say it in two sentences but if
you're going to write good copy, you
don't; 	you say it in two pages.
You want to push him to despair.
Suppressed Feelings
The third method 	is 	reminders of suppressed negative feelings about self.
Here's copy from another client of mine. Actually, I'll give you two versions of 	these
reminders 	of suppressed negative feelings about self.
I thought maybe they were right about me. Maybe I was in over my head.
What the devil was I doing here sitting in the reception room of the president of
a $100 million company waiting to tell him what to 	do?
Emotional Pain Methods
1. “All too familiar” situations.
2. 	Personal confessions the reader identifies
with.
3. 	Reminders of suppressed negative feelings
of self.
4. 	Negative imagination triggered of others’
poor perceptions of or disappointments with
them.
Page 18 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	21
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
What if he asked what university I graduated from? What if he called other
executives in? I've never felt confident speaking to groups.
Here's another version of the same copy:
I thought maybe my father had been right all along. We are not the sort of
people who ever rise to top incomes or live in the big homes in the best
communities.
What made me think I could make tens of thousands of dollars a month when
my father barely made as much working all year?
If it's in their heads, we wish to stimulate it for them.
Negative Imagination
The fourth method is 	triggering negative imagination of 	others' poor 	perceptions 	of
them. 	I just showed you that in the previous ads.
Secret 	#3: Anger, Rage and Resentment
The third 	factor 	is 	also a dark one 	– 	anger, rage and resentment.
Getting people mad and then selling to them is a great approach to selling.
For example, a video appeared of the Toronto mayor 	in addition to the 	initial crack-
smoking drunk video. 	In this one, 	he 	was 	pretending to assault somebody and
saying a bad word every three seconds and threatening the life of somebody and
running around the room screaming manically.
He is out of his mind with rage at whoever this person is and it is perhaps fueled by
booze and crack as well.
Here's one thing certain: simultaneous with that, in those minutes, he is incapable of
rational thought.
Page 19 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	22
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
 	He is incapable of anything but his anger and rage.
 	He is incapable of skepticism.
 	He 	is incapable of analytical thought.
 	He 	is incapable of critical thought.
 	He 	is incapable of adding up value and
comparing it to price.
He 	is incapable of any of that.
The mind can only hold one thing at a time, by the
way.
If 	we get them mad, they are incapable of analytical, critical, rational thought about
the proposition that we put in front of them. They are only capable of being mad.
So, we have distracted them from all of the ways that they figure out how not to buy.
Let 	me give you some examples. 	This 	is Boardroom copy 	– 	Boardroom is one of the
largest 	info-marketers there is; newsletters and books, bottom line health, bottom
line personal, bottom line business.
This is one of the great anger, rage headlines I've 	ever seen. It's Boardroom's.
"The IRS 	Has 	Had You 	Long Enough!"
It would be great with a picture of a fist around somebody's testicles; 	that would be
the perfect photo. 	It wouldn't bother me to do it but it's probably a little much for
Boardroom.
"Siphoned out of our wallets" is a piece of copy that appears.
And for what? So wasteful bureaucrats can throw our money down a rat-hole?
So money-grubbing politicians can use it to pay political favors? So IRS agents
can hound you and harass you and treat you as guilty until proven innocent? I
say 	ENOUGH.
3. ANGER, RAGE,
RESENTMENT
Everyone is angry about
something and resents
somebody. Bring it from a
simmer to a boil.
Page 20 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	23
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
This 	is what it looks like; 	it’s got a 	picture of Marty Edelston standing there with
arms folded and 	“The 	IRS 	Has Had You Long Enough!”
The solution is a book, of course, with 5,122 ways that you 	can reduce your taxes
and deprive the IRS of money.
But if I get you mad enough at the IRS you do not do a lot of critical thinking about
how many of those 5,122 	ways 	apply to you, how many are legal 	or 	what your CPA
might think about them.
You don't think about any of that.
You're just mad and you act on being mad.
How can you now act on being made mad?
Well, you can't go grab your baseball bat 	out 	of
your closet and go beat up an IRS agent.
So, the way you can act on the rage that has
been created is buy the book and you
immediately feel better 	when 	you buy the
book because you did something about being had long enough.
Understand that, 	in our world, 	purchase counts as accomplishment
and it is in our best interest to let that confusion occur.
So, the person who gets whipped up by this copy and is mad as hell at the IRS and
the money-grubbing politicians and acts on his rage by ordering the book
immediately feels better because he has done something, finally about this
deplorable situation.
 	Has 	he really done anything? No.
 	Has he changed anything? No.
 	Has he accomplished anything? No.
 	Is he likely to? 	No.
Page 21 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	24
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
90% of the books sold never have 	the shrink wrap taken off of them
but, 	at that moment in time, 	purchase was the same as
accomplishment.
It is in our best interest to let that be.
Secret 	#4: Mysticism
The fourth factor 	is 	the one we'll spend the most time on. 	It's not all that dark, it's
just fundamentally weird and it's fascinating that we all operate this way. Everyone
will deny 	they 	do but we all operate this way.
The 	fourth factor that you can use to drive a 	lot of business in our world is
mysticism in all of 	its 	various forms.
Overwhelming majorities of our population believe in secrets:
 	They believe in 	secret societies.
 	They 	believe in conspiracies.
 	They believe that there is some secret being withheld from them that if they
just had that…
So, fat people believe… 	no disrespect to any fat folk, 	if it makes you feel any better, I
once was one of you… 	fat people believe in all sorts of 	secrets and secret
conspiracies.
They believe that there are ingredients put into the food, deliberately stuck in there,
to make people fat.
They believe that there are diet secrets skinny people know that are withheld 	from
them and not told to them. They believe all sorts of these things.
Page 22 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	25
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Here is why they must; you can't help yourself if
you're fat from believing there must be a conspiracy, a
secret.
If you are poor, poor people believe that there are
secrets deliberately withheld from them by rich
people; 	that there is an entire conspiracy, 	societally
and politically structured to keep them poor. They
must believe that.
What is the alternative belief? 	“I'm a loser.”
“I'm a big fat person with zero discipline, no sense,
who cannot manage myself at all.” 	“I'm a broke person
because I am willfully stupid, lazy, useless, pitiful and pathetic.”
This is an unappealing proposition. A much more appealing proposition is that
there's a conspiracy to keep me fat and poor.
Mysticism exists because it's a viable and preferable alternative to
reality for most people in many categories.
Just so you have 	a handle, I have some facts for you.
Aliens from outer space: 	there are currently over 500 different organizations that
people who believe that there are aliens from outer space among us belong to. In
aggregate they have 16 million members.
There is, 	for example, the Ancient Astronauts Society which, of course, 	believes in
the existence of ancient astronauts. They each pay $139 a year in dues to belong to
the Ancient Astronauts Society.
The percentage of people in general, according to 	Rasmussen Surveys, who believe
in aliens among us… 	if you do a lot of coaching it's easy to believe in aliens among
us; it explains a great deal... 32% believe in aliens among us.
4. MYSTICISM
People believe in SECRETS,
in conspiracies, in
information kept from them
and in ‘mystics’ with
mysterious, super or unique
powers… and often look to
this as the answer to why
they aren’t living life as
they’d hoped.
They can, therefore, be
made to look to this for
solution, cures and
opportunities.

www.getwsodownload.com

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	26
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
The percentage of people who believe in government cover-ups of aliens among us
is 47%. Curiously, that's higher than the percentage of the people... I'm not quite
sure of that math.
Political conspiracies: 	JFK assassination, fake moon landing,
911 truthers, Obama birthers, Bilderbergs, 	one world order,
secret US internment camps, death panels.
Every single one of these things, 	it's more than 30% of the
population believes them. I don't know it it's the same 30%, I
don't know that.
There 	are entire industries around this. 	There are speaking circuits, there are
magazines, there are newsletters, there are products, there are DVDs, there are CDs.
There's an entire industry just around political conspiracies.
We have mystical powers. December 30th, year 2000, the New Orleans Saints, the
NFL team in New Orleans, 	paid a voodoo priestess to perform a pre-game ritual to
free the team from curses. New Orleans then 	won 	its 	first playoff game in 	its 	team
history defeating the Rams.
Percentage of adults who believe in 	ghosts: 34%.
American Society of Psychical Research, which is research into the paranormal like
ghost busters, has 9,000 volumes of 	research and has received over $50 million in
grants from benefactors. Mystical powers!
This is a sales letter, not from a real weird source. 	Many people 	know Nightingale-
Conant 	and have 	bought something from Nightingale-Conant. This 	is a Nightingale-
Conant sales letter and a successful one:
Journey 	into the 	Fourth 	Dimension
There's an entire
industry just around
political
conspiracies.

Scientists are baffled. How is it that some people
are able to gain access to information completely
outside their physical experience?

done it plus you'll learn how you can do it for
yourself.

Better sub-head: 	"Remote viewing, proof of non-
local 	universal consciousness.
This is a 16-page sales letter for an audio cassette
program that teaches you how to separate your mind
from your body and get psychic insight that you can use to be more successful in life
and in business.
This is a Nightingale Conant sales letter. This is not some bizarre entity, some
person in a cave somewhere.
This is going to the Nightingale Conant list with a lot of people on that list like you 	–
none 	of whom would ever admit to buying it but a whole lot of people own it;
mystical powers.
Agora is 	one of the largest info-marketers mostly in financial information, almost all
of which is based around some sort of 	“Secret 	Formula.”
The Tout industry does roughly a billion dollars a year in business; almost 50% of it
is during the football season because more people bet on that than any other sports
but it's a year round industry.
If you 	know Jim 	Cramer 	who picks stocks 	on CNBC, think of somebody just 	as
disreputable and full of shit as 	is 	Jim 	Cramer 	– 	except, 	instead of telling you what
stock to buy, they tell you which team to bet on in the Monday night game.

-- 27 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	28
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
People 	pay them money for this inside information and expert prediction about
which team to bet on. People pay this industry a billion dollars a year to tell them
whether the Rams will cover the spread on Thursday night. It is utter crap from
beginning to end.
In fact, dirty little secret, 	because 	they sell their customers yearly plans 	– 	$3,000
plan, $5,000 plan, $10,000 plan 	– 	most of them 	start the season by dragging you to a
free game selection and they get 5,000 people to call in and 	they 	give 2,500 on one
side of the game and 2,500 on the other side of the game.
On Monday, they've got 2,500 winners, they split them again on Monday night 	and
give 1,250 one side of the game, 1,250 the other side of the game. 	On 	Tuesday
morning they have 1,250 who just went two for two.
That's the business. 	Cramer's business isn't much better.
There's a 	guy called the Wall Street 	Psychic who from 2005 to 2008 was paid over
$6 million for consulting with executives of leading banks and financial firms,
making psychic predictions about what would happen in the economy. 	Mystical
powers!
Tony's fire walk. Many people 	have done the fire walk 	but did you 	know it's a
carnival trick from the 1800s?
It works otherwise it couldn't be a carnival trick. Of course it works but it's a trick.
It is no mystical power, not by the person putting you through it and not by you.
I hate to disillusion you but you have no mystical powers evidenced by the fact that
you can walk across the hot coals without burning your feet.
It has nothing to do with you or your feet. It has an enormous amount to do with the
trick itself.

www.getwsodownload.com

The Wayne Dyer 	School of Magical Thinking; 	I'm in a meeting with Wayne with
some infomercial executives 	as he is 	explaining to them how he is able to be in three
places at the 	same time.
One executive after the other went to the restroom and none of them ever came
back. I said, 	“It 	might have been better to keep that little tit-bit to yourself.”
Suppressed cures: 	there's a whole industry build around the idea that cures exist
for virtually every disease you wish to name 	– 	particularly cancer and 	AIDS 	– 	that
are being suppressed by a conspiracy of you name it, the FDA and 	Big 	Pharma.
There are DVDs selling all about the engines that run on water, the engines that run
on cat shit, the engines that run on everything but gasoline that GM has bought up in
order to lock them away in a cabinet and not let anybody…
Self-generating electricity: 	a machine that makes 	its 	own electricity; this scam has
been run over 500 recorded times, 	where investors have been brought into rooms
and shown a machine that generates 	its 	own electricity.
The first recorded one was in the 1800s, the most recent one was only three years
ago to the tune of 20-something odd million dollars.
Fountain of 	youth: 	Cryogenics, 	the 	deal where they freeze you; 	if you haven't heard
this pitch by the way, and I realize now I run the risk of insulting somebody, wait till
you hear this pitch.
I'll preface it by 	saying 	I love the network marketing industry dearly 	however…
Up until this, the pitch at which I could not keep a straight face, I was in the back of a
room the first time I heard it and, 	because I liked the people doing it, 	I ran from the
room before I burst out laughing and ruined their whole meeting.
They 	were in the bee pollen business and the problem with the bee pollen business,
if you don't know, is that it comes by taking the stuff off the feet of the bees and the
bees die when you take the stuff.

www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	30
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Nobody really likes the specter of dying bees. So 	they have this scientist on stage
showing a presentation about how, 	to make their bee pollen, they 	have a patented
process and they have this machine that the bees go through and it only takes the
pollen from the left feet of the bees, thereby no bees die 	in the making of this
product.
Now, the guy who can deliver this with a straight face 	– 	this 	is talent!
But 	I now 	found something that trumps it 	– 	H2Om water. It may look like ordinary
bottled water but oh, no it isn't.
There are five 	different kinds, including:
 	Prosperity 	Water
 	Perfect 	Health Water
The slogan is "think it while you drink it". Wait till you hear the pitch.
This is vibration hydration. While 	Prosperity 	Water is being bottled, everybody who
is bottling it is chanting a prosperity chant 	– 	hence 	“Om Water” 	– 	infusing the water
with prosperity energy so that, 	when you drink it, 	you automatically, with no effort,
no work, no change of behavior, become prosperous.
If you don't need to be prosperous, 	Perfect Health Water 	– 	no exercise, no diet, no
nothing. They all chanted the perfect health chant while they bottled the water and
now all you've got to do is drink the water.
It's only $10 a bottle.
Miracle cures: 	sharks don't 	get cancer therefore if you eat shark cartilage you won't
get cancer.
On your own time, 	dissect the complete illogic of that statement which for many
years sold tens of millions of dollars of shark cartilage. My dog does not shed, 	so if 	I
ate my dog, I 	would still have a full head of hair and no hair would have fallen out.
Same logic. Exact same logic.
Page 28 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	31
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Miracle cures: 	Dr. 	Brinkley 	– 	who 	Chip and I wrote about in Making 	Them Believe,
the book and home study course 	– 	was 	the richest 	doctor of his time; the AMA was
invented to put him out of business.
If you don't know the story, he had people coming on trains from all across the
country to his clinic spending, at the time, roughly today’s 	equivalent would be
$75,000 for an operation, 	to have goat testicles grafted to their testicles to cure their
erectile dysfunction. Grafted to, understand, we take the goat testicle… this, by the
way, is very bad news for goats.
Almost every proposition is good news for somebody, bad news for somebody else
but this is like stapling it to the outside of your testicle.
Ladies, this is the rough equivalent of a topical that makes varicose veins go away;
there's no such thing. It's an internal problem, not an external problem.
However, this preposterous cure, the first known recorded cure being sold for
erectile dysfunction; if 	you 	want the whole 	Brinkley story, 	there's a book, not ours,
called Charlatan which is a great read.
In our book it's all about the marketing of Brinkley and he was way ahead of his time
in many respects.
They came from all across the country 	– 	and 	20% 	of his clients were 	doctors;
doctors coming to have goat testicles grafted to their testicles to cure their erectile
dysfunction.
Miracle cures miscellaneous:
 	The Flat 	Earth Society which was founded in 1800, as of 1992 had 	28,000
dues paying members.
 	The amount bid on eBay for a grilled cheese sandwich bearing the image of
the Virgin Mary was $28,000.
 	2,000 per hour came in June of 1997 to see the image of the Virgin Mary in a
puddle at the subway station in Mexico City.

www.getwsodownload.com

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	32

Financial: I remind you of Bernie Madoff. A lot of people think that's new.
If you want 	some fun sometime, Google “Home-Stake Oil Swindle” 	and get the book
which has the list of everybody who invested 	in 	Home-Stake Oil which was another
Ponzi scheme.
Much 	like Madoff, the 	list is rich with people who should know better including, at
the time, the 	Secretary of the US 	Treasury who invested in Home-Stake Oil 	– 	which
was every bit as unexplainable as 	Enron.
The Enron story was actually taken from the Home-Stake Oil story, they just
substituted electricity for oil.
In order to sell Home-Stake Oil, 	they went out in the middle of the desert outside of
Palm Springs and they put purple pipes, white pipes, green pipes, turquoise pipes,
plastic pipes up out of the desert, all 	crisscrossing 	each other and they flew people
over them in helicopters and said 	“See” 	and everybody signed checks.
In 1999 a TV commercial was produced for Whiskers cat food with embedded
subliminal messages for the cat. A CEO approved this.
Percentage of the US population that believes Elvis faked his death: 	47%. My
theory is, 	after his fake death, he spent his life working as an Elvis impersonator at
Holiday Inns. It's called 	hiding in plain sight.
We could go on.
People believe this:
WHAT “THEY” DON’T WANT YOU TO KNOW
It's one of the 100 most powerful headlines in advertising. It never wears out
because people believe in this stuff.

a millionaire

great idea.

So let me take you inside the secret world of earning fast cash profits by simply
finding wholesale real estate that insiders are keeping all to themselves.
Here's the other control 	(visual only available on DVD’s), same client.
The 	headline is a swipe 	from a 20 year old infomercial.
How 	to Get a Good Second Paycheck Without a 2nd 	Job
I will read you pieces of copy:
The most amazing means of making spare time, moonlighting or weekend
income ever invented that's hidden in plain sight on every street in America,
which is why we call it hidden-in-plain-sight money. This is kept behind a
curtain where insiders easily pocket 	finder’s 	fees.
Get this mental picture:
Step into a continuous flow of hidden in plain sight money and receive large
pay checks sent by Federal Express right to your door.
Well who wouldn't want that? The mental picture of the FedEx guy bringing you
money, suspends all rational thought. Who wouldn't want that?
The next example, 	is mailed 	to investor lists, people that have accounts in which
they 	buy and sell and trade stocks (visual only available on DVD).
Ignoring 	My Last 	2 	Alerts Cost You Quick Profits Of 589% And 610%.
The undiscovered tech stock named inside is destined to become….
What tech stock insiders don't want you to know….
Gee, that sounds familiar!
Obscure 	Biotech Firm About To Unveil the Greatest Medical Breakthrough
Since 	Penicillin!

Buy this undiscovered tech stock now and every $10,000 you invest could go to
$20,000, $30,000 or even 	$40,000.
Here is a current control:
Exposed. Obama's Shocking Second Term Plan to
Micro-Manage Everyone's Home Power Use.
Literally. The Most Insidious Power Grab by Any
Government Anywhere.
It doesn't sell information, it sells a generator box that
somehow magically makes power by itself.

you a few quick headlines. 	(This is pages 28 and 29, by the
way.)
 	How 	to Tap Into Little Known Government Money to Pay Nursing Home Bills

that veterans aren't better informed of these benefits that they deserve

 	The 	Truth About Gold. 	You 	See The 	TV 	Ads, You Hear The Radio Ads, What
You Must Know Before You Buy Gold Or If You Already Own It That They
Won't Tell You
It never wears out. The secrets that 	THEY 	don't want you to know.
Secret 	#5: Revenge, Vindication, Redemption
Factor five 	is 	revenge, an enormously popular concept. It's in the 	“They All
Page 34 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 36 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	37
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Laughed…” ad 	I showed you: 	"…until I started to play,” 	then 	they all shut up, their
jaws dropped, they were amazed. That's revenge.
Who doesn't want revenge against someone?
A former girlfriend remembers Bill Gates as being boring and
having bad breath. He remembers her as not having $100
million.
Who doesn't want revenge? By somebody, your buyer feels:
 	Underestimated
 	Thought of as 	“Such things are not for you”
 	Disrespected
 	Doubted, skeptical 	about
 	Looked 	down upon
 	Put 	down
 	Treated 	as a 	“second class citizen”
 	Excluded
These feelings are held by everybody. They feel it about someone's response to
them and they wish to prove them wrong.
Revenge stories work very well in copy.
The most raucous standing ovation I have ever seen, and I have seen many, was the
delivery of a beautifully scripted story. I 	will not do 	it 	verbatim for you but, in my
youth, I was 	at a 	large Amway rally. For those of you who don't know, the Amway
system for many years included a monthly area rally and typically a superstar of the
business was the featured speaker.
This guy's name was Charlie Marsh and he was a kind of ugly, crew cut, toady
looking guy. 	An 	ex-cop, he had a good looking wife but Charlie didn't look like much.
Charlie told the story of getting in the business and, 	of course, having his wife very
skeptical and his mother-in-law very skeptical.
5. REVENGE, VINDICATION,
REDEMPTION
By somebody, your buyer
feels…. and wants to say,
“Well, how do you like me
NOW?!”
Page 35 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 37 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	38
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Charlie had a history sort of like Jackie Gleason on the Honeymooners and Al Bundy
of trying one scheme after another to make money and failing miserably. So, here
we go again.
Charlie invites, as everyone was taught to do, to his first living room meeting, all his
friends, his cop buddies and his neighbors and relatives. At 7 o'clock, his living room
was 	empty and his nose is pressed to the window waiting for someone to come and
they do not come.
Next week he called and invited everybody again to another meeting and they did
not come.
It leads up to 	hitting 	Crown Direct 	Distributor, which at the time was the highest
level in Amway, 	and taking his wife on a 21 day world cruise 	and inviting everybody
down to the docks, to the bon voyage party 	– 	all 	the 	friends, the relatives, the
neighbors, his cop buddies, everybody 	– 	to which everybody came because there
was free 	food, free booze, balloons etc.
However, they were already on the boat so that they could stand by the railing of the
boat as all their friends gathered on the docks and give them the finger.
It's a perfect message to market match. 	I have never in all my years seen an audience
erupt and stay erupted for as long a period of time as that story because every one
of them in the room shared the 	first part of that experience and it hurts.
It is emotional pain 	not just a business occurrence and Charlie was revenge and
everybody went home that night thinking about what their version of the bon
voyage party would be.
Larry King tells the story 	– 	I 	was on the 	Success 	Events with Larry for many, many
years 	– 	which again I’ll 	very much condense, about when he was on radio in Miami
early in his career.
He got 	a call from an Italian accent, bent-nosed guy explaining to him that they were
having a benefit for the local cardinal and they wanted Larry to MC. 	Larry of course
Page 36 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 38 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	39
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
agreed and when he got here the bent-nosed guys took him back stage and
explained to Larry there's only a few rules: 	No jokes about the Mafia because there's
no such thing as the Mafia; 	don't ask anybody to turn the house lights up because
there's competitors in the room and there's some folks from the government in the
back and everybody kind of knows everybody's here but they don't like to see
everybody. So don't ask them to turn up 	the lights and please finish on time.
So Larry does his thing and all he wants to do is get out of there and they are in the
back waiting for him and everybody's very pleased. 	“You did a very nice job, we're
very grateful and now we owe you a favor.”
Larry just wants to get out of there and they said no, you don't understand, we owe
you a favor and we don't like owing people favors. So what would you like?
He says the next question you will probably never be asked in your life but the next
question 	out of the guy’s mouth to me is, 	“Well, you got anybody you want
whacked?”
Larry says, I don't care who you are, at that moment in your mind, you start a list.
Now, the same thing with Charlie's bon voyage story; what occupied their minds for
the rest 	of the evening was their version of the bon voyage party.
Even 	people not yet in the business have an experience like that where they have
been dissed by their family and their friends and their coworkers and boy, they
would like vindication.
That story 	now presses everything else out of their little pointy heads. So there's no
sales resistance to what is being sold.
It's a very important principle.
Charles Atlas made his fortune 	and 	the people around him made their fortune 	with
one of the most famous of all vindication stories about the bully who kicks sand in
the face.

www.getwsodownload.com

genre in our industry of teaching men how to pick up and have sex fast with women.
This is not the 	“build a relationship” 	category here, this is not

The Amazing 	Seduction Secrets of a Skinny, Ugly,

within 20 minutes of 	meeting her without even having

This entire pitch is about a magic mental technique that is irresistible, that you can

message to learn how.

Secret 	#6: Fear / Insecurity
Factor number six 	is 	fear 	/ 	insecurity. There are many fears and everybody has at
least one. 	The most common are:
 	Fear 	of the future
 	Fear 	of change
 	Fear 	of loss
 	Fear 	of harm
 	Fear 	of a powerful enemy
 	Fear 	of regret
 	Fear 	of ill-health and infirmity
Then 	every specific target audience had 	its 	own specific fears.
Hospital executives right now, for example, are scared to death of the uncertain and
unknown ramifications of Obamacare.
That's a specific fear but these 	general ones apply at different times to just about all
of us.
Below are seven 	headlines that could be used to sell some sort of information
product about relationships to women; so 	the audience is women. 	Which do you
think would be most effective?
1. 	Improve 	Your Home Life
2. 	Improve 	Your Marriage
Page 40 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 42 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	43
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
3. 	Improve 	Your Love Life
4. 	Improve 	Your Sex Life
5. 	How 	to Keep Your Husband Interested In You Even After Years of
Marriage
6. 	Love 	Secrets, How To Keep Your Husband Interested In You Even
After Years of Marriage
7. 	Secrets 	Husbands Wish Their Wives Knew; How to Keep Your
Husband Interested In You Even After Years of Marriage
Number six is the winner, by the way.
But are 	any of those as powerful as 	the following?
 	TELL-TALE SIGNS: How to Decode the Secret Clues That Your
Husband Is Losing Interest In You
 	TELL-TALE SIGNS: How to Decode the Secret Clues That Your
Husband Is Becoming Interested In Another Woman
 	TELL-TALE SIGNS: How 	to 	Decode the Secret Clues That Your
Husband Is Thinking About Asking For 	a 	Divorce
If you had to bet $100,000 on an ad campaign and you could choose any of the
headlines you just saw from 	the first group or the second group 	– 	and you 	could
only bet on one 	– 	which set 	would you pick a headline from?
You 	better believe it’s the second group and 	anyone who 	thinks 	otherwise 	is 	sharing
the crack-pipe with the mayor from Toronto.
If you would like to know, these are real headlines from real split tests and the
winner is divorce.
Why? Because it encompasses the most fears.
Go back to our fear list; fear of the future, fear of change, fear of loss, fear of harm,
fear of a powerful enemy 	– 	why is he getting divorced? Another woman 	– 	fear of
regret, fear of ill-health and infirmity.
Page 41 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 43 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	44
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
We’ve 	got almost all of them except maybe the last.
So 	the winner is the divorce headline.
None of the ones 	in the first 	group 	come even close to the ones 	in the second 	group.
Positive motivation, negative motivation, which has more power?
Negative motivation.
A brief diversion; many 	people 	won't like that. I don't actually like it either. I would
rather just use positive motivation.
The time to use positive motivation is after you have a customer and you are now
helping them.
That's where it belongs; after the acquisition, after the sale, not before the
acquisition and not before the sale. 	All it does there is get in your way. So, many
people 	won't like it.
If it is any consolation to you whatsoever, I don't like it either.
I'm really a warm, fuzzy, happy, cheery, optimistic guy!
I am; 	I'm a 	little fur ball but I also like to sell stuff.
The second little diversion is 	you 	may 	choose not to believe it about your customers,
your target market. You will say; well, maybe but my people are not like this. My
people are smarter, my people are better educated, my people are more
sophisticated.
I’ve 	tried to drive that home to you by mentioning how many of these ads worked in
Investor’s Business Daily. Go get the 	ad rate 	card if you'd like to see the
demographics; 	they're very high, college educated, advanced degrees, high incomes
etc.
But you will 	still say, “Not my people. My people are different” 	and you will be
wrong.
Page 42 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 44 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	45
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Why 	do you believe that, 	if in fact that thought popped into your mind?
Because it's your ego talking. It's flattering to have customers who are smarter,
more sophisticated and don't behave like this. You want to believe that about your
customers. You certainly want to believe it about yourself.
However, we all operate this way. Situationally, at 	different times, we all operate
this way.
Everybody believes themselves and their customers are different and
better, too good for this.
You would be wrong.
Anxiety and security: 	This is a top half of a postcard, very successful for an
ordinary financial adviser 	– 	meaning looking for people with investable assets,
retirees with, say, $200,000, $300,000, $400,000.
The postcard looks like this:
BROKEN PROMISES 	and why you
shouldn't rely on any government
programs for your financial security or
health and wellbeing
At the top there's a picture of a little cat snuggled
up on a couch.
If 	Only You Could Feel This Secure and
Worry-Free 	(As 	the 	cat)
This 	works for an ordinary financial adviser.

The next ad I'm 	going to show you which is a successful ad 	– 	from 	Forbes, it's mine
for a client 	– 	is 	for an un-ordinary investment adviser.
This postcard is mailing to a demographic list in the
target market area of people that might only have
$200,000.
The ad I'm about to show you is running in Forbes
and it requires response only from someone who
has sold 	or is in the process of selling their company
for at least $10 million.
Would you agree that that person 	– 	this person who
has sold or is about to sell their company for $10
million or more 	– 	is 	probably smarter, more educated, more sophisticated, saner,
more rational than the ordinary rank and file, 	perhaps blue collar, 	person who might
respond to that postcard?
No, they're not. They appear to be, they’ve 	got a better suit on. They live in a better
house. The only difference between people who have a lot of money and no money,
is the people who have a lot of money, have a lot of money.
That's it. That's the only difference.
Years ago, Wesley Snipes 	is being interviewed on the Today Show, this is before he
was a tax cheat and a felon and all that stuff. So he's being interviewed on the Today
Show and the host asked him this question: 	“Do you think of yourself as an actor
first or a black actor first?”
Snipes said, 	“Well, when I get up in the morning, naked, I'm black but when I get to
the stage, I'm an actor.”
Everybody's the same, everybody's the same.
I don't care if they read Forbes or they read Trailer-Park Monthly. I've got news for
you, 	they both check their astrology forecast.
Full Ad available as Example #15 in the
Exhibits

www.getwsodownload.com

Most people though 	think, “Of 	course, the person who reads 	Forbes 	must be
smarter, more sophisticated, more rational, more everything than the person with
just $200,000 living in a little retirement community in 	Arizona.”
Everybody would agree and everybody would be wrong.
Here's the ad:
When a man sells his company, conflicting advice flies, vultures circle,
unseen peril lurks. Who can really be trusted to tell him the truth?
The guy who wrote the book.
If you intend to sell or are selling or 	have 	recently sold a company worth $10
million to $40 million or more, I have vital experience-based information that
even your most trusted advisers may not tell you and may not even know.
You are obviously an accomplished successful person or you would not have a
valuable entity to sell but you are not experienced in this and the
intermediaries and advisers directly involved in this transaction have agendas
of their own.
You will have or now have a large sum of 	money in a low-yield environment.
You may find yourself feeling lost and overly eager to get back into an active
decision making role. You will confront conflicting advice issues with people in
your life and numerous opportunities.
If not careful, you 	will put money that can no longer be replaced into situations
with unacceptable, perhaps hidden and secret risks. I have seen very successful
and smart people make critical mistakes during and after the sale of a business
and I can present those hazards for your consideration and avoidance.
There are a surprising number of pitfalls including some concealed from view
during the process of selling a company.

www.getwsodownload.com

© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
I've only read you pieces. There is only nuanced language differences between this
and all the other fear stuff I have shown you.
Secret 	#7: Greed / Sloth
The seventh 	secret 	is 	greed and sloth; 	it never goes out of style. The desire for
something for nothing 	– 	particularly money 	– 	is 	universal.
In the 1960s, the big hit in our industry 	– 	self-published 	book, full-page newspaper
ads, it's one of the great classic ads 	– 	was 	Joe Karbo's 	“The 	Lazy 	Man's 	Way to
Riches.”
Full page ads ran on the back pages of major metropolitan newspapers and business
sections of newspapers all across the country 	– 	not the newspaper in Toothless-ville
Alabama; 	the New York Times, the LA Times, the Washington Post, the Chicago
Tribune 	– 	for the 	Lazy 	Man's 	Way to 	Riches.
Anyone with enough 	brains to actually read a copy-
rich, 	full page newspaper ad, if they are engaged in
rational thought, 	knows the promise can't be true.
Almost everybody knows at least one rich person and
they read about rich people and you can't find a 	lazy
rich person. It's impossible.
Even the third generation ones who inherit it aren't lazy. They're at every party,
nightclub, boat show, blowing money like you can't imagine. You can't do that
sitting on a couch eating Cheetos.
You cannot burn through a billion dollar fortune in a single generation being lazy
about it. You've got to work at blowing that money.
You will never meet a lazy rich person. In fact, most rich people work harder and
work longer hours than most poor people.
7. GREED / SLOTH
Free, Easy…
WITHOUT doing anything
you don’t like or don’t want
to do
Page 46 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 48 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	49
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Furthermore, 	they turn everything they do into un-lazy activity. Warren Buffet
decides to play bridge, he treks and finds the number one bridge player in the
country and pays her a ton of money to teach him how to play bridge so he can win.
That's how rich people 	have hobbies.
One of the 	reasons 	I don't play golf 	is 	because, 	if I did, it would be barely a matter of
months before I would have three coaches, be scheming to get on the senior tour,
own a country club and be immersed in golf as work. I could not help 	myself. That's
what we are.
So anyone who can read a full page newspaper ad, engaged in rational thought,
knows this is not true. However, everybody wants it. Everybody would like to know
about it if it did exist and if we can get somebody to suspend 	rational thought long
enough, 	they will buy it to find out about it.
You may recall 	a 	very successful management book 	in the 	1980s, the One Minute
Manager. No-one published the One Minute Lover! But the One Minute Manager was
a big hit.
Nobody who has 	ever managed anybody 	– 	child, employee, anybody 	– 	if 	engaged in
rational thought, thinks they 	can do it in one minute a day. But 	we'd like to.
My friend Tim 	Ferris’s' Google AdWords test-driven title 	– 	the book's a lot better
than the title 	– 	the Four Hour Work Week.
We've come from 1960 to now. Nobody's gotten any smarter.
The Four Hour Work Week? Really?
Homeless people spend more than four hours at the street corner with their sign.
No-one has a four hour work week.
While driving the 	book onto the best seller list, by the way, my friend Tim was flying
across the country to go do interviews on the Today Show, doing radio interviews
from dawn until midnight, seven days a week hustle, hustle, hustle, hustling.
Page 47 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 49 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	50
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Seemingly, you could drive the Four Hour Work Week book to the best seller list
with four hour work weeks.
No, you can't but we would like this to be true. And 	if we can get somebody to
suspend rational thought long enough they will buy it to find out whether or not it
can possibly be true.
One of the all-time hit headlines 	is: 	"Lose Weight While You Sleep” or “Make 	Money
While You Sleep" 	– 	you 	could do it either way.
It's been used in both ways. It's not new.
These headlines have a 50-year history of being trotted out and 	used again and
again and again.
Lose weight while you sleep? 	You've got to have restless leg syndrome beyond
anyone's imagination to burn off a few calories while you sleep.
I sleep fast but I don't lose any weight and anyone with a brain knows.
There’s a 	current ad campaign for the stuff you sprinkle on your food so none of the
calories and the fat grams stick to you when they go through your system. 	50 million
people have bought it.
Here's how un-new that is. In 	1963, a 	full page ad 	in the 	National Enquirer had a
huge 	banana split and a gal in a bikini with a 21 inch waist next to it shrunk down 	–
so 	the banana split was bigger than she was 	– 	sprinkling powder on the banana split
because if you put this powder on it, you could eat as many of them 	as you want and
not gain any weight or inches.
1963, this is 2013, this never goes 	out 	of style. We all know better; it 	doesn't matter,
we all hope it is possible. Greed and sloth.
Page 48 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 50 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	51
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
So here's some copy, this is all copy I've written at various times for different
promotions.
It does the work for you
It does the heavy lifting for you
“For 	you” 	are two really good words. It implies you do nothing and you get a result.
Easier than any 	way of making money you've ever seen
Make 	$1,000 a day in less time than it takes to shower and dress in the morning
Guess what that does? That triggers 	a mental picture.
That headline makes somebody think about taking a shower and getting dressed
and thinking about how many minutes they take to do that and how cool it would be
to make $1,000 in that amount of time.
While 	they're thinking that, 	they can't think of anything else.
If you can click a mouse…
“Microwave 	Money” 	– 	as 	fast as microwaving a 	cup of coffee
It’s the 	same thing; guy pictures himself in front of the microwave waiting for the
coffee.
By the way, we all do that. It takes, what, a minute? And we are impatiently standing
there waiting for the coffee. A minute; 	you'd think by now… 	we can get a man to the
moon, you'd think we could reduce this a little bit.
Mail just two typed pages to 25 of your customers every day and watch a tidal
wave of extra sales flow in
The 	magic in that is, pretty much everybody thinks they can handle getting two
typed pages into an envelope.
Page 49 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 51 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	52
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
Push button profits: 	This is a letter to get joint ventures. We first used it in 2000.
We still use it today, so it's 14 	years old. 	The client's using it today 	and it 	works as
an email too, by the way.
Re: Free Money
Dear 	…
I am interested in setting up a joint venture with you offering 	….. 	to your
customers.
It will be a very 	SIMPLE 	way for you to add substantial profits to your bottom
line 	WITHOUT 	LIFTING A FINGER OR INVESTING A DIME.
I’ll 	do all the work, 	PAY ALL THE COSTS 	and make sure the project is kept on
track.
All you have to do is give me the 	OK 	to move forward 	– 	and 	deposit the
$1,000.00 	checks 	I will send you.
This will get people on the phone. If it FedExed with $100 bill attached to it, which
is how my client uses it, it really gets people on the phone.
Yet, 	if they stopped and thought about it and they tapped their own business
experience 	– 	because this is going to successful business people in a particular
industry, peers 	– 	they 	know that there's no such thing as free money.
However, who doesn't want free money?
The combination of this promise, a $100 bill attached to it, they can't think of
anything else, rational thought is gone. Critical thought is gone, hand is going to
phone, mind going nowhere.
Here's a variation on greed and sloth. This 	will make you a lot of money.
This is called 	“How to get everything you want without doing anything you
don't like or don't want to do.”
Page 50 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 52 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	53
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
That's a template for copy. All you have to do is turn the generics into specifics that
match your market.
"How to get the exact body you want without doing any exercise" etc.
I call it the 	Pizza and 	Ice 	Cream diet, lose all the weight you want on pizza and ice
cream. 	The key word here is the word 	WITHOUT.
 	“For you” 	– 	two 	key words
 	“Without” 	– 	key word
Here it is in actual use, 	this is a current piece for a client of mine 	– 	aimed 	at dentists.
It takes eight years to go to college to be a dentist. Here's what I'm putting in front
of him 	(Example #16 	is below as well as in the Exhibits):
What 	If You Really Could…
DOUBLE 	PRODUCTION
With Only Five Elegantly Simple
But Profoundly Powerful Changes
Put In Place In Just Days….
… 	without spending one red cent more on external advertising or
marketing; 	without leasing the next new pricey techno gadget; 	without
adding to staff 	or piling new marketing work onto them and facing mutiny
and without you having to become some sort of green-eyed monster, super
salesman and master closer of giant cases….
That is how to get everything you want without doing anything you don't like or
don't like to do. That's what it is.
He has eight years of college. It will fill a seminar room.
Here is some of the longer list:
Page 51 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 53 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	54
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
 	At least double your present production
 	Speed: 	see significant production increases in the first two months
 	Without 	doubling or even sharply increasing your workload. In fact, you'll
probably 	wind up working less
 	Without 	doubling your overhead. In 	fact, you will raise profitability of your
practice. It's common for net to go down as gross 	goes up. We will not put
you on that insane trajectory
 	Without 	doubling your advertising or marketing budget or activity
 	Without 	sacrificing your professionalism, 	your ethics or your reputation
 	Without 	you becoming a super salesman or master closer
 	Without 	asking your hygienist 	to think and act like a car salesman
 	Without 	pressuring staff to think and act in ways they find insulting or
unpleasant or overly resistant or want to sabotage
 	Without 	any new pricey technology
 	Without 	piling marketing work on 	your staff
You will finally create 	INCOME CERTAINITY, predictable, steady, new patient
flow you can depend on by making only five elegantly simple changes, all of
which can be fully and completely understood in two days.
You will sustain positive staff 	pride and morale 	and real teamwork and
strengthen the entire practice.
That's a good list, isn't it?
You bet it's a good list.
Conclusion
These seven factors drive our industry.
For them to be fair, 	what you are selling with them must be:
 	Able to actually deliver on the promise, 	and
Page 52 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 54 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	55
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
 	Beneficial to the people to whom you are selling it
You decide for yourself whether you are playing fair or not or whether you wish to
play fair but these are the seven factors that drive our industry.
These seven factors 	should 	be 	embedded in your sales copy, in your sales scripts, in
your webinar script, in your personal presentation.
Ethics
As I expected last night when we finished I had a couple gentle souls come who
were disturbed about where you place the ethical line in 	all of this.
So, 	just for the record, my client actually does have five elegantly simple ways to
double production in a practice without doing all that ugly stuff you don't want
to do.
Now as a copywriter, I had to help him realize he had five elegantly 	simple
things that could be done in a practice because I wrote the copy first and then
we figured out what the five elegantly simple things were, but he actually does
have them.
You could actually lose a lot of weight on a pizza and ice cream diet if you 	chose
to. First of all there happens to be low carb, low fat, gluten free pizza; 	it tastes
like cardboard but, 	if there's enough stuff on it, 	it's perfectly okay. I consume it.
There is soy ice cream and, 	if that's all you ate … you might notice by the way
that both of those items are in the NutriSystem's program … which, if you watch
the commercial, the implication is, is that you eat all the stuff in the same day.
That's not actually the case, but if you got up in the morning and you had a low
carb, 	gluten free breakfast pizza and, 	at lunch you had a protein shake and a tiny
little cookie, 	and at dinner you had another pizza and a salad and then you had a
little bowl 	of low fat, low no sugar added, nothing ice cream; and that's all you
ate compared to what most people eat, you could take weight off of them.
Page 53 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 55 of 56 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	56
© 	2014 GKIC 	Part 1
The line is … everybody draws their own line by the way. 	I will tell you what
mine and what clients’ 	are and that is, is if they follow instructions from the
deliverable, can they get the promised benefits?
Now we know most won't follow the instructions. That's not my problem.
The only question is if they follow instructions, can they get the promised
benefits?
So the job is to construct the most compelling case you can construct, which
requires you to use the seven strategies we talked about last night, 	in order to
then sell a deliverable that will actually produce the core promises if the
instructions are followed.
So for example I showed you the money hidden in plain sight story. Well, there
is money hidden in plain sight. 	It's in every house on every street that people
drive past in America and so it is all about the real estate business and very few
people understand that there is money there that they can get right now and
that they can get it with no money, no credit, no brains. They don't have to take
title, they don't understand these things.
So there is money hidden in plain sight. 	The implication people would draw
from this is different than the reality. When you say to people there's hidden in
plain sight money, they think there's a bag of money behind a tree.
That’s what’s in their head but the reality is in fact real.
So it qualifies by my line.
Page 54 of 54
www.getwsodownload.com
